name: style
description: Evaluates Lean/Mathlib coding conventions and style.

criteria.5: Follows naming convention mapping textbook items to Lean names (e.g., theorem_1_17, lemma_1_5, def_40_3); comments/docstrings indicate correspondence without polluting Lean syntax; imports minimal and relevant; uses existing mathlib definitions when available
criteria.4: Clear structure and naming; most declarations correspond cleanly to textbook items; comments/docstrings are informative; imports are largely appropriate; new definitions introduced only when justified
criteria.3: Most items can be traced back to the text; naming mostly consistent; basic organization present but could be clearer; some redundant imports or non-idiomatic re-definitions
criteria.2: Partial mapping to text but inconsistent naming conventions; comments/docstrings missing or unhelpful; imports heavy or redundant; ignores available mathlib primitives
criteria.1: Naming/structure is chaotic; cannot map Lean items to textbook statements; comments pollute Lean syntax; over-imports or redefines basic mathlib concepts

prompt_template:
Evaluate the **code style** of this Lean 4 formalization.

## Lean Code
File: `{lean_file}`

```lean
{output}
```

## Declaration Under Review
```lean
{output}
```

## Scoring Criteria (0–5)
{criteria}

Focus on: Can each Lean item be traced back to a textbook item? Does naming follow conventions (theorem_1_17, lemma_1_5, def_40_3, prop_2_12, corollary_6_8)? Are comments/docstrings informative without polluting Lean syntax? Are imports minimal? Does it reuse mathlib definitions when available?

Respond with JSON: {{"score": <0-5>, "reasoning": "<explanation>"}}
